zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Corbet於1234年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特時，他的父親Richard Corbet Justiciar of Shropshire35歲，母親Petronilla Booley18歲。他於1260年與Ida結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1300年11月14日在英國史洛普爾維姆享年66歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾阿爾波波利。

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Corbet was born in 1234, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Corbet Justiciar of Shropshire, was 35 and his mother, Petronilla Booley, was 18. He married Ida in 1260. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 14 November 1300, in Wem, Shropshire, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in Alberbury, Shropshire, England.

ko.Moreton by Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 비 오스베스트리
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Corbet(이)가 1234년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Corbet Justiciar of Shropshire(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Petronilla Booley(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1260년 Ida(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1300년 11월 14일 영국 슈럽실르 베에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 알버버이에 묻혔다.

ru.Moreton by Oswestry, Shropshire, England: моретон би освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Corbet родился в 1234 г. в местности моретон корбет, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Corbet Justiciar of Shropshire было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronilla Booley, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Ida в 1260 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 14 ноября 1300 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности альбирбури, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Corbet nació en 1234, en Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Corbet Justiciar of Shropshire, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Petronilla Booley, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Ida en 1260. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 14 de noviembre de 1300, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Alberbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
